question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a flood of valid Hedera transactions that generate oversized record files, causing the scheduler driven by `frequency = Duration.ofMillis(500L)` to repeatedly attempt and fail to process each batch, exhausting the single scheduling thread and halting all record ingestion for the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpEL expression `#{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}` evaluated at startup by injecting a near-zero duration via an externalized config source (e.g., environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_RECORD_FREQUENCY=1ms`) to cause the `@Scheduled` task in `RecordFileDownloader.download()` to spin at maximum rate, starving the JVM thread pool and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting many large transactions to the Hedera network, cause the mirror node to store raw record file bytes in the database when `persistBytes = true` is configured, exhausting database disk space and causing a non-network DoS that halts all record processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeFiles = true` is configured, submit transactions that generate large record files which are then written to the local filesystem via `Utility.archiveFile()`, exhausting available disk space and causing a non-network DoS that prevents further record file downloads and processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeSignatures = true` is configured, trigger the mirror node to write signature files for every consensus node to disk for each record file batch, causing disk exhaustion through accumulated `.rcd_sig` files and resulting in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `enabled` defaults to `true` and is mutable via `setEnabled(boolean)` to craft a scenario where the `CutoverServiceImpl.isActive()` logic permanently disables record downloading by manipulating the stream type state, causing a non-network DoS where no new record files are ever ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded `StreamType.RECORD` return from `getStreamType()` in combination with the `CutoverServiceImpl` logic to force the importer into a state where neither BLOCK nor RECORD stream is active, causing a non-network DoS where record ingestion permanently halts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions that generate record files exceeding `CommonDownloaderProperties.maxSize` (50 MiB default), cause the downloader to repeatedly skip files and fall into a retry loop that consumes CPU and scheduler threads, resulting in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum bound on `frequency` (only `@NotNull` is enforced, no `@DurationMin`) to inject `Duration.ofMillis(0)` or `Duration.ofNanos(1)` via externalized configuration, causing the `@Scheduled` task to execute in a tight loop and exhaust the scheduling thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `persistBytes = true` is set, submit transactions containing maximum-size smart contract call data to generate record files near the 50 MiB limit, causing the database to accumulate gigabytes of raw bytes per hour and exhausting database storage, resulting in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that `RecordFileDownloader.download()` is scheduled with `fixedDelay` (not `fixedRate`), meaning a slow or blocking download cycle delays the next invocation, and by submitting transactions that generate pathologically large record files, cause each download cycle to take longer than `frequency`, effectively stalling the importer indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeFiles = true`, exploit the `downloadSidecars()` path in `RecordFileDownloader` to write both record files and sidecar files to disk simultaneously, doubling the disk write rate and accelerating disk exhaustion to cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `batchSize = 25` default in `CommonDownloaderProperties` by submitting bursts of transactions that generate 25 record files simultaneously, causing the downloader to download and parse all 25 in a single batch, exhausting heap memory and triggering an OOM-based non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `setEnabled(false)` call path triggered when `endDate` is exceeded in `Downloader.verifySignatures()` to permanently disable record downloading by crafting a record file with a timestamp far in the future, causing a non-network DoS where the importer stops processing all subsequent legitimate record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that `frequency` is read via SpEL at application startup and is not re-evaluated at runtime, meaning a misconfigured near-zero frequency permanently pins the scheduler thread, and there is no runtime circuit breaker to detect or recover from this condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `persistBytes = true`, exploit the `BatchStreamFileNotifier`'s `BlockingQueue` with limited `queueCapacity` to cause the downloader thread to block indefinitely on `queue.put(streamFile)` when the parser is overwhelmed by large byte-persisted record files, resulting in a non-network DoS that freezes the entire download pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeSignatures = true`, exploit the fact that `Downloader.verifySignatures()` iterates over all `CONSENSUS_REACHED` signatures and writes each to disk, causing a file descriptor exhaustion DoS by generating record files that require signatures from a large number of consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `consensusRatio` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` (default 1/3) by submitting transactions that cause a large number of nodes to produce conflicting signatures, forcing the downloader into repeated verification loops that exhaust CPU and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the interaction between `frequency = Duration.ofMillis(500L)` and the `BatchStreamFileNotifier`'s blocking queue, where the downloader produces files faster than the parser consumes them, causing the queue to fill and the downloader thread to block, halting all record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeFiles = true`, exploit the `Utility.archiveFile()` call to write record files to a path derived from the stream filename timestamp, and by submitting transactions with crafted timestamps, cause files to be written to unexpected filesystem locations, filling inodes or specific partitions and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StreamType.RECORD` path in `CutoverServiceImpl.isActive()` to trigger a state where the cutover logic continuously switches between RECORD and BLOCK stream types on every poll cycle, causing excessive CPU consumption and database queries that result in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `persistBytes = true`, submit transactions that generate record files containing maximum-size contract bytecode in sidecar files, causing the importer to load both the record file bytes and sidecar bytes into heap memory simultaneously, triggering an OOM-based non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the lack of a minimum frequency constraint (unlike `CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval` which has `@DurationMin(seconds = 1)`) to configure `frequency` to `Duration.ofMillis(1)`, causing the scheduler to invoke `download()` 1000 times per second and exhaust all available CPU cores?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when `writeFiles = true` and `writeSignatures = true` are both enabled, exploit the combined disk write amplification (record file + all node signature files per batch) to exhaust disk space at a rate proportional to `batchSize * nodeCount`, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `downloadRatio = 1.0` default in `CommonDownloaderProperties` to force the downloader to attempt downloading record files from all available consensus nodes simultaneously, exhausting the thread pool and causing a non-network DoS through thread starvation?
